skill.name: amazon-product-search-api-skill
skill.description: Automate Amazon product search and data extraction. Extract product titles, prices, ratings, reviews, sales estimates, and availability from Amazon search results.
skill.summary: Extract structured Amazon product data via API for market research and competitive analysis.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Security analysis confirms this skill is a legitimate Amazon product data extraction tool using BrowserAct's API service. Network access is intentional and documented. Credentials are handled securely via environment variables. The static analyzer flagged numerous patterns as suspicious, but manual review reveals these are false positives: backticks in SKILL.md are code examples in markdown documentation, not shell executions; keywords like 'Bearer' triggered weak crypto false positives; and env access detections reflect proper credential handling. Risk level set to 'low' due to third-party API dependency and network outbound requests.

low_findings.title: Third-Party API Integration
low_findings.description: Script makes outbound HTTP requests to api.browseract.com. This is intentional for product data retrieval. Service endpoint is hardcoded (api.browseract.com). This is expected behavior for a web scraping API wrapper.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network access is documented, intentional, and serves legitimate product search purpose. Service endpoint is a known third-party API.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Credential Access (SKILL.md Documentation)
low_findings.description: SKILL.md references BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable for configuration. Documentation only, not actual credential access in skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Documentation references environment variable for user guidance. No actual credential exfiltration or unsafe handling.

content.user_title: Automate Amazon product search and data extraction
content.value_statement: Gathering Amazon product data manually is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automates product search, extracting titles, prices, ratings, reviews, and availability data in structured format for analysis.
actual_capabilities: Search Amazon by keywords and brand filters
Extract product titles, URLs, prices, and ratings
Get review counts and estimated monthly sales
Check product availability and shipping info
Search in multiple languages for localized results
limitations: Requires a BrowserAct API key for authentication
Data extraction speed depends on BrowserAct service
Does not bypass Amazon CAPTCHA or IP restrictions

use_cases.title: Market research and competitive analysis
use_cases.description: Extract product data for specific brands to analyze market trends, pricing strategies, and competitive positioning. Useful for product managers and market researchers.

use_cases.title: Price monitoring and inventory tracking
use_cases.description: Monitor product prices and availability over time. Track discount patterns and stock status for specific brands or categories.

use_cases.title: Product catalog building
use_cases.description: Gather product titles, URLs, and specifications to build or update product databases and catalogs for comparison shopping or affiliate sites.

prompt_templates.title: Basic product search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Amazon for wireless earbuds from Sony and extract up to 50 products with their prices, ratings, and review counts.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner use case with keyword and brand filter

prompt_templates.title: Localized search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Amazon in Japanese for the latest laptop accessories and list the top 20 products with their prices and availability status.

prompt_templates.title: Best seller tracking
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all Best Seller products in the electronics category for Samsung brand, including prices and monthly sales estimates.

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive data extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for smartphone products with detailed info including: title, price, rating, reviews, monthly sales, availability, shipping location, and whether marked as Best Seller.

output_examples.input: Search Amazon for laptop stands from Herman Miller, extract 30 products with prices and ratings.
output: 1. Herman Miller Sayl Chair - $1,095.00 - Rating: 4.5 stars (234 reviews) - Best Seller
2. Herman Miller Aeron Chair - $1,395.00 - Rating: 4.7 stars (512 reviews) - Available
3. Herman Miller Embody Chair - $1,595.00 - Rating: 4.6 stars (189 reviews) - In Stock

output_examples.input: Find Sony headphones in Japanese, show top 10 with prices and availability.
output: 1. Sony WH-1000XM5 - ¥35,000 - Rating: 4.8 (1,200+) - 在庫あり (In Stock)
2. Sony WF-1000XM4 - ¥24,000 - Rating: 4.7 (890+) - 在庫あり (In Stock)

best_practices: Store your BrowserAct API key as an environment variable (BROWSERACT_API_KEY) before running searches
Start with smaller product limits (20-50) to test results before scaling to larger datasets
Use the brand filter parameter to focus searches on specific competitors for targeted analysis
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode API keys directly in scripts or configuration files
Avoid requesting extremely large product counts in a single query as this may timeout
Do not use this skill to bypass Amazon access restrictions or scrape beyond allowed limits

faq.question: How do I get started with this skill?
faq.answer: You need a BrowserAct API key. Set it as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable, then specify your search keywords and optional brand filter when calling the skill.

faq.question: What data fields can I extract from Amazon product searches?
faq.answer: You can extract: product title, URL, rating score, review count, monthly sales estimate, current price, list price, delivery info, shipping location, Best Seller status, and availability.

faq.question: Can I search Amazon in different languages?
faq.answer: Yes, set the language parameter to supported values: en, de, fr, it, es, ja, zh-CN, or zh-TW to get localized search results.

faq.question: How many products can I extract in one search?
faq.answer: The default limit is 50 products. You can specify a higher maximum count, but very large requests may take longer to complete or timeout.

faq.question: What happens if my API key is invalid?
faq.answer: The script will detect authorization errors and report them clearly. It will not retry with an invalid key. Check your BrowserAct dashboard for a valid API key.

faq.question: Does this skill handle CAPTCHA or IP blocking?
faq.answer: No, the skill relies on BrowserAct's infrastructure to handle CAPTCHA and regional restrictions. You do not need to manage these manually.
